task: I’m putting together a quick set of Chromebook help notes for someone who keeps asking me whether they can use Firefox instead of Chrome and whether they can still get to their saved Apple passwords when they’re in Chrome, so could you check a few things in a real browser for me? Start on Mozilla’s official Firefox site and open the actual Chromebook or ChromeOS instructions so you can confirm whether Firefox can really be installed there and what Mozilla says the install method is; leave that page open because I want the official wording as a reference. Then go to the Chrome Web Store and find the official iCloud Passwords extension from Apple, open the actual listing page, and verify from the page itself that it’s for accessing iCloud passwords in Chrome; keep that tab open too so I can see the publisher and listing URL. Since these notes should also cover a Google Docs issue they run into all the time, use Google Search to look up reliable troubleshooting for images in Google Docs that show an exclamation mark or refuse to load, then open a useful result and pull out the recommended fixes. After that, open the UserTesting contributor sign-in page so I can confirm a normal login screen is reachable for a site where saved credentials might matter, then in another tab open Patreon’s homepage just to verify ordinary browsing works there too and leave both tabs open so I can compare them. Finally, go to YouTube, open the video titled "Youtube Rewind 2011," start playback, and tell me what you see in the first moments so I know media playback works in the browser. At the end, give me a concise summary of what you confirmed on each site, including the Firefox Chromebook answer, the iCloud Passwords extension name and listing URL, the Google Docs image fixes, and whether UserTesting, Patreon, and YouTube all behaved normally.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully navigated to the official Mozilla support page ("Run Firefox on ChromeOS"). In its final summary, it correctly confirmed that Firefox can be installed on Chromebooks/ChromeOS and specified the method (enabling Linux support and installing the DEB package via Mozilla's APT repository). The page was kept open as requested.

R2: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Chrome Web Store, searched for "iCloud Passwords," and opened the official listing page for the "iCloud Passwords" extension published by Apple Inc. The agent correctly identified the extension name ("iCloud Passwords"), the purpose (to fill passwords from iCloud Keychain in Chrome), and provided the correct listing URL (`https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/icloud-passwords/pejdijmoenmkgeppbflobdenhhabjlaj`) in the final summary.

R3: success - The agent successfully searched for the Google Docs image issue, opened a relevant Google Docs Editors Community thread, and navigated to an official Google Drive troubleshooting page. In the final summary (Step 40), the agent provided a comprehensive list of concrete recommended fixes (e.g., checking internet connection, clearing cache, using supported browsers, toggling offline access, and specific workarounds like re-uploading images). This satisfies the requirement to find guidance and summarize concrete fixes.

R4: success - The agent successfully navigated to the UserTesting contributor sign-in page (https://network.usertesting.com/). The snapshot in step 29 confirms the presence of a "Participant log in" heading, along with email and password textboxes and a "Log in" button. The agent's final summary explicitly confirms that the UserTesting page was reachable and displayed the expected login fields.

R5: success - The agent successfully opened the Patreon homepage in its own tab (tab index 5, URL https://www.patreon.com/). The snapshot of this tab confirms the presence of Patreon branding, including the "Patreon" logo link, "Log in" and "Get Started" buttons, and a "Find a Creator" search box. The agent's final summary explicitly confirms that the Patreon homepage loaded normally.

R6: success - The agent successfully navigated to YouTube, searched for "Youtube Rewind 2011," and opened the video. The screenshots and action history confirm that the video was playing (timestamp 0:08 / 2:19) and the agent provided a description of the first moments of the video, satisfying all requirements of the rubric.

R7: success - The agent provided a comprehensive final summary that addresses all six required points:
1. Firefox on Chromebook: Confirmed it can be installed via Linux/APT repository.
2. iCloud Passwords extension: Provided the name and the correct URL.
3. Google Docs image troubleshooting: Extracted multiple relevant fixes.
4. UserTesting: Confirmed the sign-in page is reachable.
5. Patreon: Confirmed the homepage works.
6. YouTube: Confirmed playback works and described the initial visual content.

The summary is concise and covers all required site-specific findings without omissions.
